concepts.label: Cross-Docking

concepts.label: Scan-Level Visibility
concepts.description: Item-level or pallet-level tracking with scan events at every handling point

items.q: What freight fits in a box truck?
items.a: Multi-stop retail delivery routes (4-12 pallets per stop). Store replenishment at locations without dock access.

items.q: How many pallets can a box truck carry?
items.a: A box truck typically handles 4-12 pallets with a max weight capacity of 8,000-16,000 lbs.

items.q: When should I NOT use a box truck?
items.a: Full truckload volumes exceeding 12 pallets. Long-haul linehaul over 500 miles.

items.q: Does WARP offer box truck shipping?
items.a: Yes. WARP operates a fleet that includes box truck capacity, matched to shipment size through our right-sized asset approach.

rendered.body_text:
Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes

A box truck handles 4-12 pallets with a capacity of 8,000-16,000 lbs. Interior dimensions: 16-26 ft length, 7-8 ft height.

Best fit for: Multi-stop retail delivery routes (4-12 pallets per stop); Store replenishment at locations without dock access.

Get an instant box truck freight rate at wearewarp.com/quote.
rendered.primary_content_html:
Box Truck Overview

Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes

Mode: Cargo Van / Box Truck

Box Truck Specifications

SpecificationValue
Capacity Pallets4-12
Capacity Lbs8,000-16,000
Interior Length Ft16-26
Interior Height Ft7-8
Door Typeroll-up with optional liftgate

Best Fit Freight for Box Truck

  • Multi-stop retail delivery routes (4-12 pallets per stop)
  • Store replenishment at locations without dock access
  • Regional distribution within 300-mile radius
  • Freight requiring liftgate for ground-level delivery

When to Choose a Different Vehicle

  • Full truckload volumes exceeding 12 pallets
  • Long-haul linehaul over 500 miles
  • Oversized or flatbed-required freight
  • Bulk commodities requiring maximum cubic capacity

Solutions Using This Equipment

  • Store Replenishment — Scheduled multi-stop retail freight delivery using LTL and right-sized assets
  • Pool Distribution — Consolidated freight pooling from distribution centers to regional delivery zones
  • Vendor Consolidation — Multi-vendor pickup consolidation into single linehaul shipments
  • Zone Skipping — Bypass carrier sort hubs by injecting freight closer to final delivery zones

Related Network Capabilities

  • Right-Sized Assets — Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency
  • Flexible Routing — Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements
  • Cross-Docking — Direct transfer of freight between inbound and outbound vehicles without warehousing
  • Scan-Level Visibility — Item-level or pallet-level tracking with scan events at every handling point

Ship with Box Truck Today

Get Instant Quote Book a Fit Call
